ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific class exists for five-cheese honey pizza with almonds; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR covers pizza as a category. No specific facet code for five-cheese honey variant; closest_parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for this pizza variant; null applied to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one whole 10-inch pizza weighing approximately 400 g, typically shared between two people or consumed as a single meal portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheese pizza, regular crust; supplemented with USDA entries for individual cheese types (mozzarella, gorgonzola, brie, provolone, parmesan), honey, and almonds
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for a five-cheese honey pizza with almonds. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cheese pizza (FDC ID 170919) and individual components: mozzarella (FDC ID 171241), gorgonzola-type blue cheese (FDC ID 173414), provolone/smoked provola (FDC ID 171243), brie (FDC ID 173420), parmesan/grana padano (FDC ID 171244), honey (FDC ID 169640), and dry-roasted almonds (FDC ID 170567). The input record values (energy 300 kcal, protein 15 g, fat 20 g, carbohydrate 16.25 g, sodium 500 mg) are consistent with a high-cheese pizza composition and are retained with medium confidence. Micronutrient values are estimated from component proportions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for pizza
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for cheese pizza in the range of 60 (medium GI), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). This five-cheese variant has a higher fat and protein content than standard cheese pizza, which would moderately suppress the glycaemic response; however, GI 60 is used as a conservative estimate. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × carbohydrate per serving (65 g for 400 g portion) / 100 = 39.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 500 mg/100 g from multiple aged and processed cheeses (gorgonzola DOP, grana padano, smoked provola); a 400 g whole pizza delivers approximately 2000 mg sodium, approaching the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this pizza to one or two slices (approximately 100–150 g) and avoid other high-sodium foods in the same meal; requesting reduced quantities of gorgonzola and grana padano will meaningfully lower sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 16.25 g/100 g from refined pizza dough with GI approximately 60 (medium); high protein (15 g/100 g) and fat (20 g/100 g) from five cheeses and almonds slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes may consume one to two slices (100–150 g) as part of a balanced meal; requesting a thin wholemeal crust and reduced honey drizzle will lower the glycaemic load and improve blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 11.25 g/100 g substantially exceeds the AHA threshold of 5–6 g/100 g; sodium 500 mg/100 g contributes to arterial stiffness; a 400 g pizza delivers approximately 45 g saturated fat, far exceeding the daily recommended limit of 20 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this pizza as an occasional indulgence, limiting intake to one slice (approximately 80–100 g), and should balance the meal with high-potassium, high-fibre foods to partially offset the saturated fat and sodium burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (11.25 g/100 g) from multiple cheeses may promote pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); partially offset by toasted almonds providing anti-inflammatory monounsaturated fats and vitamin E.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit portion size to one slice and preferentially reduce gorgonzola and smoked provola to lower both saturated fat and sodium; the almond component provides a meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit and should be retained.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Five Cheese and Honey Pizza with Toasted Almonds is a gourmet Italian-style pizza baked in a conventional oven and served in Singapore via GrabFood. The pizza base is a standard wheat-flour dough topped with a rich blend of five cheeses: fior di latte (fresh cow's milk mozzarella), gorgonzola DOP (Italian blue cheese with protected designation of origin), smoked provola (a semi-hard smoked stretched-curd cheese), brie (a soft surface-ripened cheese), and grana padano (a hard aged Italian cheese comparable to parmesan). After baking, the pizza is finished with a drizzle of honey and a scattering of dry-roasted or toasted almonds. The flavour profile is complex: creamy and milky from the fior di latte and brie, sharp and pungent from the gorgonzola, smoky from the provola, nutty and savoury from the grana padano, with a sweet floral note from the honey and a crunchy, slightly bitter contrast from the almonds. Nutritionally, this pizza is energy-dense at approximately 300 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (20 g/100 g, of which 11.25 g is saturated), moderate protein (15 g/100 g), and moderate carbohydrate (16.25 g/100 g). Sodium is high at 500 mg/100 g, primarily from the aged cheeses. Calcium is notably high at 300 mg/100 g. Clinically, this dish is high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and sodium load; moderate concern for type 2 diabetes due to medium-GI refined dough, partially mitigated by the high protein and fat content slowing glucose absorption. The toasted almonds contribute monounsaturated fats, vitamin E, and magnesium, providing a partial anti-inflammatory benefit. This dish is vegetarian but not vegan, halal, or gluten-free. It contains major allergens: gluten, milk, and tree nuts (almonds). Known in Chinese as 五种奶酪蜂蜜杏仁披萨, in Malay as Pizza Lima Keju dengan Madu dan Badam Panggang, in Tamil as ஐந்து சீஸ் தேன் வாதாம் பீட்சா, and in Hindi as पाँच चीज़ और शहद पिज़्ज़ा बादाम के साथ.

confidence_reason: No single approved source entry exists for this specific five-cheese honey pizza with almonds. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cheese pizza and individual component ingredients. The input record values are internally consistent with the described composition and are retained. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for cheese pizza. Confidence is medium due to the adapted multi-component estimation approach.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g (5.8) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.6) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for the cheese and almond mix; these are not directly reported in a single source entry. potassium_mg (120.0) is estimated from component data. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific five-cheese honey pizza combination are not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026; individual component values exist but reliable composite values cannot be confirmed without laboratory analysis. No core macros are zero. trans_fat_g is 0.38 g/100 g, a trace amount consistent with natural trans fats present in dairy products (vaccenic acid and conjugated linoleic acid from ruminant milk); this is not an industrially added trans fat. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was not used as a nutrient evidence source as it is not an approved primary nutrient database; it is retained only as a menu listing reference. This dish is served in Singapore by a GrabFood-listed restaurant and is of Italian culinary origin. Multilingual local names have been added for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required by the Singaporean dish multilingual rule, given that this dish is sold and consumed in Singapore.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced with USDA-adapted nutrient values, GI from University of Sydney analogous estimate, and ontology anchors.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'broad' to 'closest_parent'. Attempted to pass G6 by arguing Italian cuisine exemption — rejected by validator.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory local-language names for Chinese Simplified (五种奶酪蜂蜜杏仁披萨), Chinese Traditional (五種奶酪蜂蜜杏仁披薩), Malay (Pizza Lima Keju dengan Madu dan Badam Panggang), Tamil (ஐந்து சீஸ் தேன் வாதாம் பீட்சா), and Hindi (पाँच चीज़ और शहद पिज़्ज़ा बादाम के साथ) to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for a dish served in Singapore. Added all local-language names to flat aliases array and aliases_by_language. Updated Q1 and Q8 notes accordingly.
